statement: A frozen analysis contract plus isolated subagent responsibilities will reduce drift and make iterative V3 analysis substantially more reliable than the current partially shared workflow.
rationale: The repo already has a stable cache and investigation entrypoint; the remaining failures are mostly coordination and promotion problems rather than missing infrastructure.
keyAssumptions: All analysis passes can share one stable snapshot set and one canonical derived-table layer.
validationCriteria: Subagents can work in parallel without conflicting edits by staying on separate layers: stats, figures, narrative.
Every figure and table can be traced back to a manifest entry and a frozen input contract.
critiquePoints: A formal contract adds process overhead and may slow quick exploratory iterations.
questions: What exact fields should the contract pin to make cache drift impossible to miss?
